source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure Cloud Asset Inventory Is Enabled

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure Cloud Asset Inventory Is Enabled
input_row.description: GCP Cloud Asset Inventory is services that provides a historical view of GCP resources and IAM policies through a time-series database. The information recorded includes metadata on Google Cloud resources, metadata on policies set on Google Cloud projects or resources, and runtime information gathered within a Google Cloud resource. Cloud Asset Inventory Service (CAIS) API enablement is not required for operation of the service, but rather enables the mechanism for searching/exporting CAIS asset data directly.
input_row.rationale: The GCP resources and IAM policies captured by GCP Cloud Asset Inventory enables security analysis, resource change tracking, and compliance auditing. It is recommended GCP Cloud Asset Inventory be enabled for all GCP projects.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console Ensure that the Cloud Asset API is enabled: 1. Go to API & Services/Library by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/library 2. Search for Cloud Asset API and select the result for Cloud Asset API 3. Ensure that API Enabled is displayed. From Google Cloud CLI Ensure that the Cloud Asset API is enabled: 1. Query enabled services: gcloud services list --enabled --filter=name:cloudasset.googleapis.com If the API is listed, then it is enabled. If the response is Listed 0 items the API is not enabled.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console Enable the Cloud Asset API: 1. Go to API & Services/Library by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/library 2. Search for Cloud Asset API and select the result for Cloud Asset API 3. Click the ENABLE button. From Google Cloud CLI Enable the Cloud Asset API: 1. Enable the Cloud Asset API through the services interface: gcloud services enable cloudasset.googleapis.com Default Value: The Cloud Asset Inventory API is disabled by default in each project. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/asset-inventory/docs Additional Information: Additional info • Cloud Asset Inventory only keeps a five-week history of Google Cloud asset metadata. If a longer history is desired, automation to export the history to Cloud Storage or BigQuery should be evaluated. Users need not enable CAI API if they don't have any plans to export.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/asset-inventory/docs Additional Information: Additional info • Cloud Asset Inventory only keeps a five-week history of Google Cloud asset metadata. If a longer history is desired, automation to export the history to Cloud Storage or BigQuery should be evaluated. Users need not enable CAI API if they don't have any plans to export.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control for ensuring the Cloud Asset Inventory API is enabled can be fully verified using GCP APIs and CLI commands.
- The audit section provides a specific `gcloud` command to check if the `cloudasset.googleapis.com` service is enabled, which indicates that this check is API-driven and does not require manual intervention.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_cloud_asset_inventory_api_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP CLI Command:** Use the command `gcloud services list --enabled --filter=name:cloudasset.googleapis.com` to check if the Cloud Asset API is enabled.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - **Pass:** If the service `cloudasset.googleapis.com` is listed in the output, indicating it is enabled.
  - **Fail:** If the service is not listed, indicating it is disabled.
- **Resource to Enumerate:** Enumerate all projects in the GCP environment to ensure the Cloud Asset API is enabled for each project.
- **Validation:** The presence of `cloudasset.googleapis.com` in the list of enabled services confirms compliance.
- This automated check can be integrated into CSPM tools to continuously monitor the status of the Cloud Asset Inventory API across all projects. 

**SOURCE:** CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
